meaning: n.少量(搀加物),冲撞,破折号,锐气,精力,干劲vi.猛掷,冲撞vt.泼溅,使猛撞,搀和,使破灭,使沮丧,匆忙完成
examples: Oh, Tim! I'm sorry but I have to dash.
He cut quite a dash in his uniform.
The rug adds a dash of colour to the room.
Many tourists splash-and-dash their way through the hamam experience, leaving immediately after their treatment.
Most are a mix of visionary and hustler, with a dash of attention deficit disorder thrown in.
